_records.meter_creation_explanation: This is a unique utility billing meter object because the document shows a clearly identified service recipient and separate meter-specific invoice totals for multiple meters/services over a billing period. The bill includes explicit meter numbers and usage-based charges, which indicate distinct metered services rather than a single cumulative charge. The provider is the City of Windom, and there is no evidence of a passthrough provider, so the meter is treated as a delivery meter.

This is a unique meter because the invoice separately lists Water/Sewer service with a distinct meter number and charge total, indicating a separate metered utility measurement. The presence of a specific meter number and service-specific charges shows this is not a cumulative or shared charge. Sewer is modeled as a distinct meter object because the bill presents water/sewer billing separately and the service is explicitly associated with that meter grouping.

This is a unique meter object because the invoice separately identifies a second Water/Sewer meter number with its own charge total, which indicates a distinct metered service. The meter is unique by its labeled meter number and separate billed amount. The sewer component is represented as a separate meter object tied to the same water usage context per the bill's water/sewer grouping.

This is a unique meter because the invoice separately lists Water Only service with its own meter number and charge total. The distinct meter number and separate line item demonstrate a separate metered service period and usage measure. It is therefore treated as a separate water meter object.
